planned_changes: Produce CSV exports of every sheet in the workbook (RawInputs.csv, Calculations.csv, DecisionTable.csv) and include them in the attempt deliverables.

Create a plain-text final_decision_table.csv that lists each run, the numeric values used (yield, impurity), the computed decision (Accept/Reject), and the exact cell citations for each referenced value (sheet!cell).
Add a PDF snapshot of each worksheet showing formulas' cached values to make results human-verifiable even if formula parsing fails.

Modify build_formulation_screen.py (or generation pipeline) to include both formula cells and adjacent cached-value columns or cell comments that contain the citation strings used by the decision logic.
Update final_message.txt to include a one-paragraph summary with explicit example cell citations for at least one row and confirm that CSV/PDF exports are attached.

Run the end-to-end precheck and validator locally (or as part of CI) to ensure openpyxl (or the chosen preview tool) can parse at least the CSV copies and that the final_decision_table.csv matches the DecisionTable sheet.
Attach agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json to the deliverables if not already explicit, ensuring reproducibility and traceability of steps.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve artifact verifiability and traceability so grader/verifier can perform full semantic checks (openpyxl parsing failed). Add parseable exports and explicit cell-citation mapping to enable automated verification of calculations and decisions.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The baseline produced the required three-sheet formulation_screen.xlsx and accompanying logs. Deterministic precheck passed (artifact present). However, the grader could not programmatically open/inspect the .xlsx (preview error: openpyxl unavailable), so semantic verification was partial (0.75). To obtain full credit and allow automated verification, deliverables must include parseable exports and an explicit, human-readable mapping from final decisions to exact cell citations.
